expected_findings.description: db.ts lacks parameterized query support — query() and transaction() only accept raw SQL strings, forcing all callers to concatenate user input. This is the systemic root cause of SQL injection across the codebase.
SQL injection in users.ts getUser(): userId is interpolated directly into a SELECT query string
Sensitive data exposure in users.ts getUser(): returns all fields including password_hash and api_key
SQL injection in users.ts searchUsers(): searchTerm is interpolated into LIKE clause without parameterization or escaping of SQL wildcards
SQL injection in users.ts updateUserRole(): userId and newRole interpolated into transaction queries
Timing side-channel in users.ts verifyPassword(): uses == for password comparison instead of constant-time compare

Broken authentication in users.ts verifyPassword(): compares password_hash (a hashed value) directly to the raw plaintext password using ==, which always fails because a hash will never equal the original plaintext — no user can ever log in
Path traversal in uploads.ts saveUpload(): user-controlled filename passed to path.join without validating the resolved path stays within the upload directory
No file size limit in uploads.ts saveUpload(): arbitrarily large uploads accepted
Path traversal in uploads.ts saveUploadExclusive(): same path.join vulnerability as saveUpload, user-controlled filename can escape upload directory
TOCTOU race condition in uploads.ts saveUploadExclusive(): gap between existsSync check and writeFileSync allows race condition
Insecure session token generation in session.ts: uses Math.random() which is not cryptographically secure
Session born expired in session.ts createSession(): expiry computed as Date.now() minus 24 hours instead of plus
SQL injection in session.ts createSession(): userId and token interpolated into audit log INSERT query
Synchronous session cleanup in session.ts: iterates entire session map synchronously, blocking the event loop
Transaction does not ROLLBACK on failure in db.ts: if any query in transaction() throws, the error propagates without issuing ROLLBACK, leaving the database connection in a failed transaction state
Audit logging in session.ts createSession() is fire-and-forget: the query() call is not awaited in the synchronous function, so audit INSERT failures are silently dropped as unhandled promises

saveUploadExclusive in uploads.ts does not create the upload directory: unlike saveUpload which has a mkdirSync guard, saveUploadExclusive calls writeFileSync directly and throws ENOENT if the directory does not exist
LIKE wildcard characters (%, _) not escaped in users.ts searchUsers(): user input containing SQL wildcards produces unexpected pattern matches independent of the SQL injection vulnerability
Sensitive data exposure in users.ts searchUsers(): uses SELECT * and returns full User objects including password_hash and api_key to all callers
No filename length validation in uploads.ts saveUploadExclusive(): saveUpload checks filename.length against MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH but saveUploadExclusive omits this check entirely

Sensitive data exposure via console.log in db.ts: pool.query() logs every SQL statement to console including interpolated user data (session tokens, user IDs, search terms, role values), leaking sensitive information to stdout/server logs

Synchronous file I/O in uploads.ts blocks the Node.js event loop: both saveUpload() and saveUploadExclusive() use writeFileSync, existsSync, and mkdirSync which block during disk I/O, stalling all concurrent request processing especially for large uploads
